text: When designing REST APIs, clear planning is essential for successful integration. Your endpoints should be intuitive and consistent, using nouns instead of verbs – for example, `/users` instead of `/getUsers`. Proper status codes help clients understand responses: 200 for success, 201 for resource creation, 400 for bad requests, and 404 when resources aren't found. Documentation is critical, as even the best-designed API is useless if developers can't understand how to use it. Tools like Swagger or OpenAPI make this easier. Additionally, version your API from the start using URLs like `/v1/users` to avoid breaking existing clients when making future changes. Good design saves time for everyone.
